title: cyanus triumfettii subsp. dominii
intro: Cyanus triumfettii subsp. dominii, also known as the Bluebells of Italy, is a subspecies of the Cornflower plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities, which are derived from its flavonoid and terpenoid compounds. The health benefits of Cyanus triumfettii subsp. dominii include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and flu. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
